facts.text: Everglades city was founded in 1906 by Ted Smallwood, whose historic Smallwood Store still stands as a living testament to pioneers.
The first permanent settler, William Smith Allen, arrived in 1873 on Potato Creek, later renamed Allen River.
The historic Bank of Everglades Building is undergoing a multi‑phase rehabilitation that began in January 2026, with foundation work nearly complete.
In 1960 Hurricane Donna’s winds and flooding destroyed 153 homes in Collier County, highlighting the region’s vulnerability to tropical storms.
The Everglades Digital Library hosts a comprehensive film and web documentary titled 'Water's Journey: Everglades,' showcasing the area’s ecological story.
